name: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 25.05.2026

origin.filename: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 25.05.2026.pdf

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Government of India Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare
texts.text: Government of India Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Weekly report/ Minutes of the Crop Weather Watch Group (CWWG) meeting as on 25.05.2026
texts.text: Weekly report/ Minutes of the Crop Weather Watch Group (CWWG) meeting as on 25.05.2026

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Key Insights
texts.text: Key Insights

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. Rabi Season harvest and Summer Season Sowing Status:
texts.text: 1. Rabi Season harvest and Summer Season Sowing Status:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  As on May 22, 2026, the area sown under summer crops is 86.02 lakh ha as compared to 83.50 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.  Of this, area of 31.05 lakh ha has been sown under Rice this year. There is also an increase in the area sown under Maize by about 1.50 lakh ha so far from the last 5-years average.
texts.text:  As on May 22, 2026, the area sown under summer crops is 86.02 lakh ha as compared to 83.50 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.  Of this, area of 31.05 lakh ha has been sown under Rice this year. There is also an increase in the area sown under Maize by about 1.50 lakh ha so far from the last 5-years average.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  As on 22.05.2026, 97.92% of area  has  been  harvested  for  Rabi  crops  all  over  India.  The  harvest  of pulses,  oilseeds,  and  Shri  Anna-cum-coarse  cereals  has  been  completed;  79.71%  area  of  Rice  and 99.50% area of Wheat has been harvested for Rabi crops 2025-26.
texts.text:  As on 22.05.2026, 97.92% of area  has  been  harvested  for  Rabi  crops  all  over  India.  The  harvest  of pulses,  oilseeds,  and  Shri  Anna-cum-coarse  cereals  has  been  completed;  79.71%  area  of  Rice  and 99.50% area of Wheat has been harvested for Rabi crops 2025-26.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Weather Update:
texts.text: 2. Weather Update:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Temperature:  Maximum  temperature  is  likely  to  be  above  normal  in many  parts  of  Northwest India, Chhattisgarh, East India, Northeast India and northern part of Coastal Andhra Pradesh. Heat wave to severe heat wave conditions likely to prevail over northwest and central India during many days of the week.
texts.text: Temperature:  Maximum  temperature  is  likely  to  be  above  normal  in many  parts  of  Northwest India, Chhattisgarh, East India, Northeast India and northern part of Coastal Andhra Pradesh. Heat wave to severe heat wave conditions likely to prevail over northwest and central India during many days of the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Rainfall  Distribution:  From  March  01-  May  24  2026,  the  All-India  cumulative  rainfall  is  1% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India: 5%; (ii) Northwest India: (-) 8%; (iii) Central India: 19%; and South Peninsula: (-) 3%.
texts.text: Rainfall  Distribution:  From  March  01-  May  24  2026,  the  All-India  cumulative  rainfall  is  1% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India: 5%; (ii) Northwest India: (-) 8%; (iii) Central India: 19%; and South Peninsula: (-) 3%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c) A fresh Western Disturbance likely to affect Northwest India from 28th May, 2026.
texts.text: A fresh Western Disturbance likely to affect Northwest India from 28th May, 2026.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: d) Isolated  heavy  to  very  rainfall  likely  over  Kerala  &  Lakshadweep,  Tamil  Nadu,  Northeast  & adjoining east India during next 4-5 days.
texts.text: Isolated  heavy  to  very  rainfall  likely  over  Kerala  &  Lakshadweep,  Tamil  Nadu,  Northeast  & adjoining east India during next 4-5 days.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: e) Isolated light to moderate rainfall with thunderstorm, lightning & gusty winds likely over Vidarbha during  26th-28th;  Konkan  &  Goa  on  27th  &  28th;  Madhya  Maharashtra  during  25th-28 th , Marathawada during 25th-27th May; Punjab, Haryana & Chandigarh, West Uttar Pradesh during 28th-30th; East Uttar Pradesh during 25th-29th and Rajasthan on 30th May.
texts.text: Isolated light to moderate rainfall with thunderstorm, lightning & gusty winds likely over Vidarbha during  26th-28th;  Konkan  &  Goa  on  27th  &  28th;  Madhya  Maharashtra  during  25th-28 th , Marathawada during 25th-27th May; Punjab, Haryana & Chandigarh, West Uttar Pradesh during 28th-30th; East Uttar Pradesh during 25th-29th and Rajasthan on 30th May.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: f) Heat wave to severe heat wave conditions likely to continue to prevail over Central & Northwest India  during  next 7  days;  East &  adjoining Peninsular  India  during  next  3-5  days. Reduction  in maximum temperatures likely from 29th May onwards.
texts.text: Heat wave to severe heat wave conditions likely to continue to prevail over Central & Northwest India  during  next 7  days;  East &  adjoining Peninsular  India  during  next  3-5  days. Reduction  in maximum temperatures likely from 29th May onwards.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Inputs Situation
texts.text: 3. Inputs Situation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  21  May  2026,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  166  important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 60.830 billion cubic meter, which is 33.14% of total live storage capacity (183.565 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 111.95 percent of last  year's  storage  and  124.39  percent  of  the  average  storage  of  the  last  ten  years  during  the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  21  May  2026,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  166  important reservoirs across the country (monitored by CWC) is 60.830 billion cubic meter, which is 33.14% of total live storage capacity (183.565 BCM). The current year's storage is nearly 111.95 percent of last  year's  storage  and  124.39  percent  of  the  average  storage  of  the  last  ten  years  during  the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Groundwater  Levels :  In  Post-monsoon  2025,  In  January,  2026,  around  75.8%  of  the  country's monitoring stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are observed in around 13.5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.
texts.text:  Groundwater  Levels :  In  Post-monsoon  2025,  In  January,  2026,  around  75.8%  of  the  country's monitoring stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are observed in around 13.5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The  deeper  groundwater  level  in  the states  like  Western  Rajasthan,  Punjab,  Haryana  and Chandigarh  during  January,  2026  has  been  significantly  influenced  by  the  over  extraction  of groundwater compared to the recharge in the area, indicating negative water balance.
texts.text:  The  deeper  groundwater  level  in  the states  like  Western  Rajasthan,  Punjab,  Haryana  and Chandigarh  during  January,  2026  has  been  significantly  influenced  by  the  over  extraction  of groundwater compared to the recharge in the area, indicating negative water balance.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level in January, 2026 when compared to the decadal mean water level of 2016-2025.
texts.text:  About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level in January, 2026 when compared to the decadal mean water level of 2016-2025.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The  Groundwater  Drought  Indices  indicate  that  in  January,  2026,  groundwater  conditions  across India remained largely stable, with 90.30% of monitoring stations falling under normal condition in respect of Ground Water Drought. However, localized deficits were observed in several regions notably in Arunachal Pradesh, Himachal Pradesh, Meghalaya, Nagaland.
texts.text:  The  Groundwater  Drought  Indices  indicate  that  in  January,  2026,  groundwater  conditions  across India remained largely stable, with 90.30% of monitoring stations falling under normal condition in respect of Ground Water Drought. However, localized deficits were observed in several regions notably in Arunachal Pradesh, Himachal Pradesh, Meghalaya, Nagaland.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Fertilizers: The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Fertilizers: The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Pests  &  Diseases :  Intensity  of  pests  and  diseases  at  some  fields  has  been  reported  to  be  above Economic Threshold Level for Litchi (Stinkbug and Mealy bug) in Bihar and for Rice (Neck Blast) in Assam. The Advisory has been issued by the survey team to the farms and regular monitoring is being carried out. The overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level
texts.text: Pests  &  Diseases :  Intensity  of  pests  and  diseases  at  some  fields  has  been  reported  to  be  above Economic Threshold Level for Litchi (Stinkbug and Mealy bug) in Bihar and for Rice (Neck Blast) in Assam. The Advisory has been issued by the survey team to the farms and regular monitoring is being carried out. The overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 5. Crops whose Mandi Wholesale prices are below MSP:
texts.text: 5. Crops whose Mandi Wholesale prices are below MSP:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: All the commodities are currently going below MSP during this week.
texts.text: All the commodities are currently going below MSP during this week.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. RAINFALL
texts.text: 1. RAINFALL

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  During the week (14 May - 20 May 2026), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 14%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st March to 20 May 2026) is higher than LPA by 2%.
texts.text:  During the week (14 May - 20 May 2026), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 14%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st March to 20 May 2026) is higher than LPA by 2%.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Rainfall Forecast:
texts.text: Rainfall Forecast:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Overall,  thunderstorm  activity  is  likely  to  continue  over  the  east  &  northeast,  and  the  southern peninsular India during some/many days of the week.
texts.text: Overall,  thunderstorm  activity  is  likely  to  continue  over  the  east  &  northeast,  and  the  southern peninsular India during some/many days of the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Overall, rainfall is likely to be normal to above normal over most parts of the country except over some parts of the Western Himalayan region during the week.
texts.text: Overall, rainfall is likely to be normal to above normal over most parts of the country except over some parts of the Western Himalayan region during the week.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: TEMPERATURE: MIN & MAX
texts.text: TEMPERATURE: MIN & MAX

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Week 1 (22 May - 28 May 2026):
texts.text: Week 1 (22 May - 28 May 2026):

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  No significant change in maximum temperatures likely over many parts of Northwest India till 28th May and gradual fall by 3-5°C on 29th & 30th May.
texts.text:  No significant change in maximum temperatures likely over many parts of Northwest India till 28th May and gradual fall by 3-5°C on 29th & 30th May.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Gradual  rise  in  maximum  temperatures  by  2-3°C  likely  over  Punjab  till  26th  May;  no  significant change on 27th & 28th May and gradual fall by 3-5°C on 29th & 30thMay.
texts.text:  Gradual  rise  in  maximum  temperatures  by  2-3°C  likely  over  Punjab  till  26th  May;  no  significant change on 27th & 28th May and gradual fall by 3-5°C on 29th & 30thMay.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  No significant change in maximum temperatures likely over Central India till 28th May and gradual fall by 2-4°C on 29th & 30th May.
texts.text:  No significant change in maximum temperatures likely over Central India till 28th May and gradual fall by 2-4°C on 29th & 30th May.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Details of each Segment
texts.text: Details of each Segment

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  No significant change in maximum temperatures likely over East India till 26th May and gradual fall by 2-3°C during 27th - 30th May.
texts.text:  No significant change in maximum temperatures likely over East India till 26th May and gradual fall by 2-3°C during 27th - 30th May.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  No significant change in maximum temperatures likely over the rest parts of country till 30th May 2026.
texts.text:  No significant change in maximum temperatures likely over the rest parts of country till 30th May 2026.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Week 2 (29 May - 04 June 2026):
texts.text: Week 2 (29 May - 04 June 2026):

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Low  to  moderate  probability  of  Heatwave  conditions  likely  over  some  parts  of  northwest  India (Punjab, Haryana, Rajasthan, Uttar Pradesh, Jammu & Kashmir, southern parts of Himachal Pradesh & Uttarakhand) during some/many days of the week.
texts.text:  Low  to  moderate  probability  of  Heatwave  conditions  likely  over  some  parts  of  northwest  India (Punjab, Haryana, Rajasthan, Uttar Pradesh, Jammu & Kashmir, southern parts of Himachal Pradesh & Uttarakhand) during some/many days of the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Above  normal  (+1.6 to +3.0°C) to appreciably above normal (+3.1  to  +5.0°C)  maximum temperatures are likely over some parts of northwest India, and over some parts of northeast India. Below-normal  maximum  temperatures  (-1.6  to  -  3.0°C)  are  likely  across  some  parts  of  South Peninsular India,  and  near-normal  maximum temperatures  are  likely  across  the  rest  of  the  country during the week.
texts.text:  Above  normal  (+1.6 to +3.0°C) to appreciably above normal (+3.1  to  +5.0°C)  maximum temperatures are likely over some parts of northwest India, and over some parts of northeast India. Below-normal  maximum  temperatures  (-1.6  to  -  3.0°C)  are  likely  across  some  parts  of  South Peninsular India,  and  near-normal  maximum temperatures  are  likely  across  the  rest  of  the  country during the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Low probability of warm night conditions is likely over Punjab, Haryana, and adjoining West Uttar Pradesh on some days of the week.
texts.text:  Low probability of warm night conditions is likely over Punjab, Haryana, and adjoining West Uttar Pradesh on some days of the week.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Advance of Southwest Monsoon 2026:
texts.text: Advance of Southwest Monsoon 2026:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  The  Northern  Limit  of  Monsoon  continues  to  pass  through  7°N/60°E,  7°N/70°E,  7°N/75°E, 8°N/80°E, 10°N/85°E, 13.5°N/90°E, and 17°N/95°E as on 24th May 2026.
texts.text:  The  Northern  Limit  of  Monsoon  continues  to  pass  through  7°N/60°E,  7°N/70°E,  7°N/75°E, 8°N/80°E, 10°N/85°E, 13.5°N/90°E, and 17°N/95°E as on 24th May 2026.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Conditions  are  favourable  for  further  advance  of  southwest  monsoon  into  some  more  parts  of southwest  &  southeast  Arabian  Sea,  Comorin  Area,  southwest,  southeast  &  eastcentral  Bay  of Bengal and remaining parts of Andaman Sea during next 2-3 days.
texts.text:  Conditions  are  favourable  for  further  advance  of  southwest  monsoon  into  some  more  parts  of southwest  &  southeast  Arabian  Sea,  Comorin  Area,  southwest,  southeast  &  eastcentral  Bay  of Bengal and remaining parts of Andaman Sea during next 2-3 days.

texts.orig: 2. CROPS
texts.text: 2. CROPS

texts.text: Rabi Crops - Area coverage

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Rice : About 31.05 lakh ha area coverage under Summer Rice has been reported as compared to 32.42 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text:  Rice : About 31.05 lakh ha area coverage under Summer Rice has been reported as compared to 32.42 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Pulses : About 27.91 lakh ha area coverage has been reported compared to 27.26 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text:  Pulses : About 27.91 lakh ha area coverage has been reported compared to 27.26 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig:  Oilseeds : About 11.04 lakh ha area coverage has been reported compared to 9.58 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text:  Oilseeds : About 11.04 lakh ha area coverage has been reported compared to 9.58 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. RESERVOIR STORAGE
texts.text: 3. RESERVOIR STORAGE

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As on 21 May, 2026, the current live storage in 166 reservoirs was 60.830 BCM (33.14%) of total live capacity of 183.565 BCM.
texts.text: As on 21 May, 2026, the current live storage in 166 reservoirs was 60.830 BCM (33.14%) of total live capacity of 183.565 BCM.

texts.orig:  129 reservoirs having storage more than 80% of normal storage.

texts.text:  26 reservoirs having storage 51% to 80%.

texts.text:  11 reservoirs having storage upto 50%.
